key_points: Off-campus housing quality is consistently poor across multiple complexes
Dorm noise and courtesy issues are a real problem, especially freshmen
Parking so bad students miss class; lot closures made it worse
key_quotes: People have been saying this about the cottages for over a decade
The build quality is very poor, the appliances are cheap and we found a dirty sock in our living room on move in day
App should focus on more parking and housing instead of other new campus facilities

key_points: Football culture is huge; the Michigan upset is still campus legend
Frat scene exists but has documented assault and harassment incidents
Strong outdoor/nature social culture; Boone town life is a real draw
key_quotes: I was there. Freshman year, had been there for all of a week and this happened. Completely winded myself sprinting from the Legends viewing party to our football field for the goal post
Owen smith Boone nc app state, we all know he kicked a girl in the face when she confronted him for calling her friend the f slur
App use to be a hippie school now it's a bro/hipster school. Fits the current trend of rich douches moving here

key_points: Dining hall food is overpriced and underwhelming — $10 for small portions
Complaints about food quality stretch back decades, not a new problem
Local spots like Black Cat Burritos are universally preferred over campus dining
key_quotes: They'll charge $10 for something as depressing as this and a drink isn't even included. Chipotle is 7 minutes down the road
Dang. Nothing's changed but the weather and my shoe size... The subpar food [was a con] back when I went... and I graduated in the 90's!
My daughter graduated from there 2022... Yeah I didn't like any food in that whole town

key_points: Missing one week of class can crater grades — system punishes illness
Snow and campus beauty are genuine stress relievers students genuinely value
Overdose risk on campus prompted student-led naloxone and harm reduction efforts
key_quotes: I've been sick for the past 2 months straight... I had a week so bad I had to miss class. Immediately my grades dropped from all 90+ to under 80
Remember to prioritize your mental health as you finish out the semester. If you're feeling overly anxious, take a break from studying and take a walk
Providing our peers with education, training, test kits, and naloxone could save a stranger or friends life at a frat party

key_points: Grad assistants had promised tuition waivers revoked mid-process by provost
Cap and gown fees include forced $17 shipping with no pickup option
UNC system underfunds App State per student; school compensates by growing enrollment
key_quotes: The written job listing explicitly promised a tuition waiver as part of the compensation... Earlier in March, App State's Provost implemented a school-wide policy eliminating [it]
17 dollar shipping on top of an 80 dollar cap and gown why in the world are these people so greedy
App has consistently been in the bottom of funding per student. The only way to get more is more students

key_points: AI tools being pushed inconsistently — some profs ban it, others assign it uncritically
Student abilities described as declining sharply over the last 15 years by faculty
Advising errors caused a student to get a graduation-short-credits email weeks after graduating
key_quotes: I know someone who had to take his calculus exam on the computer. There were ad breaks. He could not skip the ads. They took up exam time
The quality of the student has declined, and the pandemic exacerbated it... boy-oh-boy have student abilities plummeted
I graduated this fall semester... There has always been a lot of confusion... only for a couple months after I complete the courses to be told it doesn't count

key_points: Administration expanded enrollment aggressively without matching infrastructure or funding
Housing office refused to act on a trespassing roommate situation to avoid 'escalation'
Faculty housing promised as affordable was priced equal to or higher than market rate
key_quotes: Thank you to whoever is responsible for the enshittification of my bachelor's degree
Housing wouldn't do anything because it would 'escalate' the situation
Call it affordable housing, get good press, then just don't make it affordable

key_points: Campus is genuinely beautiful — snow photos go viral every winter for a reason
Boone's mountain location means real snow driving hazards every winter
Town has a beloved local food and outdoor culture that alumni miss for years
key_quotes: I miss app. I'm in Charlotte now so there's not these quiet moments, or any snow
God I loved and love Boone so much, but I've seen better driving in small Albanian towns
I was an international student and studied at App State for three years. It's the place I will definitely visit again before I die

key_points: Alumni report strong outcomes across diverse fields despite school's regional reputation
One alum joke-thread showed barefoot hippies become Fortune 50 managers and SVP directors
Degree value threatened by accreditor switch to DeSantis-aligned body; employers may not recognize it
key_quotes: I went to App from 2003-2007, would love to do it all over again but this time get a degree that is worth something
Manage a team of data analysts for a fortune 50 company [on being asked where barefoot App grads ended up]
The thing that Republicans don't understand is that while they can cheapen their schools' accreditation... that devalues the degree

key_points: In-state tuition is genuinely affordable by national standards
Hidden fees pile up: overpriced dining, towing, mandatory software, cap and gown shipping
Expanding enrollment without infrastructure investment means you're paying more for a crowded experience
key_quotes: Can't make more money without more students
The university doesn't give a shit about anything other than sportsball at this point
Dang. Nothing's changed but the weather and my shoe size... The subpar food, the advisors, the parking — all still cons from the 90s

key_points: Strong community identity built around mountains, football, and outdoor culture
Campus culture shifting from hippie/outdoorsy to bro-heavy as enrollment expanded
Hurricane Helene aftermath showed students and community genuinely rally together
key_quotes: App State use to be a hippie school now it's a bro/hipster school
They shut down school for a few weeks after the hurricane... those kids that stayed behind and helped the locals are a blessing
Forever Mountaineers

key_points: **Safety: Reported rape surge on campus; one survivor said her report was literally lost by the university**
**Administration: Tuition waivers revoked after grad assistants signed leases and made moving arrangements**
**Accreditation: NC law forces App State to switch to politically motivated accreditor, threatening degree recognition**
key_quotes: I was raped my first year and reported it, they actually LOST my report… so nothing was done
I signed a lease, made multiple down payments, and made travel arrangements to move my life to Boone... [then the tuition waiver was revoked]
App state use to be a hippie school now it's a bro/hipster school... Do I have concerns about political games leading to worse outcomes for students? Yes, certainly

key_points: Black Cat Burritos is a beloved local institution — every alum mentions it
Belk Library late-night has a cult following; the coffee is genuinely good
Blue Ridge Conservancy land access and nearby outdoor recreation is world-class
key_quotes: Go to Blackcat Burrito on Depot St
Nothing like sitting on floor 2 sipping that joe and grinding... the coffee was so good. I took the gourmet stick boy coffee for granted till I went other places
All my homies love and donate to the BRC, fuck yeah
